MAX_KEY_HASH.brief: The maximum number of hash buckets allocated for key hashing operations.
MAX_KEY_HASH.details: MAX_KEY_HASH is a compile-time constant set to 1024, representing the upper limit for the number of hash buckets in key hashing structures. It is used throughout the codebase to define the size of hash tables managing key-value pairs, ensuring consistent and efficient distribution of entries. This constant influences the performance characteristics of hash-based lookups and is referenced in hash functions, data structures, and algorithms that handle key hashing.
MAX_KEY_HASH.notes: As a constexpr, MAX_KEY_HASH is optimized for compile-time evaluation, promoting performance and consistency. Its fixed size simplifies memory management and hash distribution assumptions.
MAX_KEY_HASH.rationale: This variable exists to standardize the size of hash tables used for key hashing, providing a balance between memory usage and lookup performance across the application.
MAX_KEY_HASH.name: MAX_KEY_HASH

PAGELEN.brief: A constant defining the default number of lines displayed per page in text output interfaces.
PAGELEN.details: PAGELEN is a compile-time constant set to 22, representing the standard number of lines shown per page during text display operations. It serves as a default value used in functions such as 'do_channels' to control pagination and is assigned to character-specific variables like 'lines' to customize individual display preferences. This constant ensures consistent pagination behavior across the application and provides a reference point for adjusting display length settings.
PAGELEN.notes: As a constexpr, PAGELEN is optimized for compile-time use, ensuring efficient and consistent pagination across the codebase.
PAGELEN.rationale: This variable exists to standardize the number of lines displayed per page, facilitating uniform user interface behavior and simplifying adjustments to pagination settings throughout the application.
PAGELEN.name: PAGELEN

usages.class_character, Character::Character(): Assigned to the 'lines' variable to set the number of lines per page for the character's display.
usages.channels_8cc, void do_channels(Character: Used to compare with the character's 'lines' setting to display the number of lines or buffering status.

MAX_ATTR.brief: The maximum number of attribute slots that can have bonus modifiers applied.
MAX_ATTR.details: MAX_ATTR specifies the upper limit for attribute indices that are eligible for bonus modifiers within the system. It is set to one more than the highest attribute index that supports such modifiers, facilitating inclusive iteration and validation in functions that copy, display, or validate character attributes. This constant ensures consistent handling of attribute slots across the codebase and helps prevent out-of-bounds errors during attribute processing.
MAX_ATTR.notes: The value is defined as a compile-time constant, reflecting the fixed maximum number of attribute slots supported. It is used as an upper bound in loops and validation checks related to character attributes.
MAX_ATTR.rationale: This variable exists to define a clear boundary for attribute-related operations, ensuring that all relevant attribute slots are processed correctly and efficiently, and that the system remains robust against invalid attribute references.
MAX_ATTR.name: MAX_ATTR

usages.area__handler_8cc, void clone_mobile(Cha: Defines the upper limit for attribute copying in the loop, controlling how many attributes are duplicated.
usages.act__info_8cc, void do_bonus(Character *: Defines the upper limit for attribute iteration, controlling which attributes are checked and displayed.
usages.namespaceaffect, void affect::modify_cha: Defines the upper bound for valid attribute locations, used to validate 'location' values.

DEFENSE_MOD_MEM_SIZE.brief: The total size in bytes of the defense modification array used for character defense adjustments.
DEFENSE_MOD_MEM_SIZE.details: DEFENSE_MOD_MEM_SIZE specifies the total memory size allocated for the defense_mod array, which consists of 32 integer elements. It is calculated as the size of an integer multiplied by 32, ensuring consistent memory allocation and boundary checks across the codebase. This constant is utilized in functions that allocate or access the defense_mod array, such as during character modification processes, to prevent buffer overflows and maintain data integrity. By defining this size explicitly, it supports safe and predictable management of defense-related modifications within the system.
DEFENSE_MOD_MEM_SIZE.notes: This constant assumes that each element in the defense_mod array is of type int and that the array contains exactly 32 elements. It is primarily used for memory allocation and boundary validation in defense modification routines.
DEFENSE_MOD_MEM_SIZE.rationale: DEFENSE_MOD_MEM_SIZE exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to handle the memory footprint of the defense_mod array, ensuring safe access and consistent sizing across different modules that modify or read defense attributes.
DEFENSE_MOD_MEM_SIZE.name: DEFENSE_MOD_MEM_SIZE

usages.namespaceaffect, void affect::modify_cha: Specifies the size of the defense_mod array, used for allocation and boundary checks.

APPLY_CACHE_MEM_SIZE.brief: Defines the total size of the cache used for storing attribute bonus modifications.
APPLY_CACHE_MEM_SIZE.details: APPLY_CACHE_MEM_SIZE specifies the amount of memory allocated for the apply_cache array, which holds attribute bonus modifiers for characters. It is calculated as the product of the size of an integer and MAX_ATTR, representing the maximum number of attribute slots that can be modified. This size ensures that the cache can accommodate all attribute modifications without overflow, facilitating efficient memory management during attribute application processes. The variable is primarily used during cache allocation and validation in functions that modify character attributes, such as affect::modify_char.
APPLY_CACHE_MEM_SIZE.notes: The size is directly tied to MAX_ATTR, ensuring consistency between the maximum attribute slots and the cache size, which helps prevent buffer overflows and maintains system robustness.
APPLY_CACHE_MEM_SIZE.rationale: This variable exists to allocate sufficient memory for caching attribute modifications, enabling efficient and safe application of attribute bonuses across all relevant attribute slots for characters.
APPLY_CACHE_MEM_SIZE.name: APPLY_CACHE_MEM_SIZE

usages.namespaceaffect, void affect::modify_cha: Defines the size of the apply_cache array, used for allocation and boundary validation.
